task_completion.description: Verify that the new role 'ROLE_GIS_READER' has been created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'ROLE_GIS_READER' in the Roles list under the Security -> Users, Groups, Roles section of the GeoServer web interface.

task_completion.description: Verify that the user 'gis_reader' has been created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'gis_reader' in the Users list under the Security -> Users, Groups, Roles section.

task_completion.description: Verify that the user 'gis_reader' is assigned to the 'ROLE_GIS_READER' role.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the user details or role assignment screen for 'gis_reader' and verify that 'ROLE_GIS_READER' is listed under their assigned or active roles.

task_completion.description: Verify that data security is configured to grant 'ROLE_GIS_READER' read access to the 'ne' workspace.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look in the Security -> Data section for a rule matching workspace 'ne', layer '*', and access mode 'r' (read) or the pattern 'ne.*.r', and verify it is assigned to 'ROLE_GIS_READER'.

task_completion.description: Verify that service security is configured to allow 'ROLE_GIS_READER' access to the WMS service.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look in the Security -> Services section for a rule associated with the 'wms' service (e.g., 'wms.*') and verify that 'ROLE_GIS_READER' is granted access.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the GeoServer web interface to perform the configurations.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the GeoServer web administration GUI (http://localhost:8080/geoserver/web/) being actively used, rather than terminal commands like curl or editing XML configuration files directly.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not just type the expected outcomes into a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The configuration must be shown inside the actual GeoServer application interface, not as text written in a notepad, terminal echo, or script file.

integrity.description: Verify the screenshots show genuine interaction with the GeoServer application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for standard GeoServer UI elements like the left-hand navigation menu (Server, Layer Preview, Security, etc.) and standard form fields for users, roles, and security rules.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires configuring GeoServer (v2.25.2). Expected user is 'gis_reader' (password: 'GisR3ader2024!'). Expected role is 'ROLE_GIS_READER'. Data security must grant read access to the 'ne' workspace (pattern 'ne.*.r') for the new role. Service security must allow WMS service access for the new role.
